message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

re difficulties in copy-paste, scrolling selection, etc: someone else had this to say (read and respond):


Right — once you need pane-bounded selection and autoscroll, the outer terminal’s selection is the wrong layer. Panopt has to own selection for that pane.

Thought for a few seconds

Yeah — then outer WezTerm selection is not acceptable. It is screen-cell selection across the whole terminal viewport. It cannot know Panopt’s pane boundaries or Panopt’s off-screen pane scrollback.

For your use case, Panopt needs to own selection inside the terminal pane.

Best design

For each TerminalPane, maintain:

PTY output stream
  -> terminal emulator state
    -> pane-local screen grid
    -> pane-local scrollback
    -> pane-local selection

Then implement mouse selection against that pane’s terminal buffer, not against the outer WezTerm buffer.

That gives you:

drag starts inside Claude pane
  selection constrained to that pane

drag above/below pane
  autoscroll that pane’s terminal scrollback

Cmd-C / Ctrl-Shift-C / copy command
  copies Panopt terminal-pane selection

Ctrl-C
  still sends interrupt to Claude
This means Panopt must include a terminal emulator

Not just a PTY bridge.

You need something equivalent to:

TerminalPane
  PTY child process
  VT/xterm parser
  screen grid
  alternate screen
  scrollback
  selection model
  mouse protocol encoder
  renderer clipped to pane rectangle

Libraries that can do the terminal-state part:

libvterm
alacritty_terminal
wezterm/termwiz ecosystem
vte
xterm.js

If Panopt is itself a terminal TUI running inside WezTerm, this is nested emulation:

WezTerm
  Panopt TUI
    Panopt TerminalPane emulator
      PTY
        Claude Code

That is absolutely possible. It is how tmux/screen-style nesting works conceptually, except you need pane-local selection and copy.

Mouse routing

Panopt should decide mouse behavior per terminal pane:

Normal click/drag
  if child app has mouse reporting enabled:
    send mouse events to Claude
  else:
    start Panopt terminal-pane selection

Modified click/drag, e.g. Alt-drag or Cmd-drag
  force Panopt selection even if Claude enabled mouse reporting

Do not use Shift if the outer WezTerm intercepts it. Pick a modifier that reaches Panopt reliably in your environment, or add a mode toggle.

Example:

Ctrl-Space      enter pane selection mode
Esc             exit pane selection mode
drag            select within current pane
wheel/edge drag autoscroll pane scrollback
Enter/Cmd-C     copy selection

A mode toggle is more reliable than modifier detection across terminals.

Autoscroll while selecting

Implement it inside Panopt:

on mouse drag:
  if y < pane.top:
    scroll selection pane up
  if y > pane.bottom:
    scroll selection pane down
  update selection endpoint using new scroll offset

Keep selection endpoints in terminal-buffer coordinates, not visible screen coordinates:

Selection {
  anchor: BufferCell { row, col }
  cursor: BufferCell { row, col }
}

Then rendering clips/highlights the visible portion.

Copy to macOS clipboard

For local:

mac → WezTerm → panopt → claude

Panopt can copy by emitting OSC 52 to the outer terminal. WezTerm supports writing to the clipboard via OSC 52, while clipboard reads are restricted/opt-in because they are riskier.

For remote:

mac → WezTerm → ssh → panopt → claude

OSC 52 is still the right mechanism: remote Panopt writes an OSC 52 sequence to stdout, SSH carries it to local WezTerm, and WezTerm sets the macOS clipboard. This is the standard “copy from remote terminal app to local clipboard” path.

So Panopt copy should be:

selected_text = terminalPane.selectionText()
emit_osc52(selected_text)

Also provide fallback:

write selected text to a temp file
show copied text in a copy buffer
pipe to pbcopy only when local macOS
pipe to wl-copy/xclip/xsel on Linux if available

But OSC 52 is the right default for wezterm → ssh → remote app.

Do not depend on Cmd-C reaching Panopt

In this stack:

mac GUI shortcut layer
  -> WezTerm
    -> terminal byte stream
      -> Panopt

Cmd-C is normally consumed by WezTerm/menu handling. Treat it as unreliable for Panopt-internal copy.

Use a terminal-native Panopt binding:

Ctrl-Shift-C   copy Panopt selection
Enter          copy while in selection mode
y              yank while in selection mode

But Ctrl-Shift-C may collide with WezTerm’s own default copy binding. WezTerm binds CTRL-SHIFT-c to copy by default.

So I would use:

Ctrl-Space     enter selection mode
arrows/hjkl     move selection cursor
v               begin/extend selection
y               copy via OSC 52
Esc             exit

And optionally support mouse drag inside that mode.

Practical recommendation

For Panopt terminal panes:

Default mode:
  keyboard/mouse go to Claude as faithfully as possible

Pane selection mode:
  Panopt captures mouse/keyboard
  selection is constrained to focused pane
  dragging past pane edge autoscrolls pane scrollback
  y or Enter copies via OSC 52
  Esc returns control to Claude

That avoids breaking WezTerm, works over SSH, respects pane boundaries, and allows off-screen selection.

So the answer is: yes, but Panopt must implement terminal-pane-local selection and clipboard export. The outer terminal cannot do what you want.

attachment.content:
- verify-all: Run the full check + clippy sweep across the workspace AND the WASM plugin (panopt-zellij). Use after non-trivial edits, before declaring work done, or when asked to verify the repo builds cleanly. Captures the dual-target dance (workspace builds for host, panopt-zellij builds only for wasm32-wasip1) so you don't have to remember the right manifest-path/target each time.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

lastPrompt: re difficulties in copy-paste, scrolling selection, etc: someone else had this to say (read and respond):   Right — once you need pane-bounded selection and autoscroll, the outer terminal’s selection…

aiTitle: Implement pane-local terminal selection and copy

content.text:
## Response to the commenter

The argument is internally consistent but it's solving a problem panopt doesn't have, because it mis-models the stack. The commenter assumes panopt is a TUI that owns PTYs directly:

```
WezTerm → panopt TUI → PTY → Claude
```

The actual stack is:

```
WezTerm → Zellij → (plugin pane | viewer pane | form panes | agent panes running Claude)
```

Zellij **is** the terminal emulator. It already provides everything in the "Best design" block: per-pane PTY child, VT parser, screen grid, alternate screen, pane-local scrollback, selection model, mouse encoder, pane-clipped renderer. We don't add a third emulator inside an already-nested stack — we use Zellij's.

Mapping their recommendations onto panopt's reality:

- **"Pane-local selection constrained to the focused pane"** — Zellij's scroll/copy mode does exactly this. `Ctrl-S` (default) enters it; selection is bounded to that pane's grid and reaches into that pane's scrollback. The mouse-drag-without-mode that we currently surface is the outer WezTerm modifier-drag, which the commenter is right to dismiss as the wrong layer for off-screen selection.
- **"Autoscroll while dragging across the pane edge"** — also already in Zellij's scroll mode. The lever we haven't pulled is making it the first-class gesture for "I want to copy something the agent printed five screens ago," with a friendlier entry binding than `Ctrl-S`.
- **"Mode toggle is more reliable than modifier detection"** — agreed, and that's literally what Zellij scroll mode is. The mode-vs-modifier question is a UX choice on top of what Zellij already provides, not an architecture choice.
- **"Copy to mac clipboard via OSC 52, works over SSH"** — already the design. `copy_command` is wired to a generated shell script that writes OSC 52 to the Zellij client's PTY fd (the `/proc` fd hunt at `crates/panopt/src/up.rs:370-466`), falling back to `/dev/tty`. The last week of commits (`faf2b8a`, `34f2e2c`, `26eb521`) were specifically about getting that byte stream to actually reach WezTerm — a plumbing problem, not an architectural one. Cmd-C unreliability is exactly why we bind `Ctrl-Shift-C` (commit `755295b`).
- **"Don't depend on Cmd-C"** — agreed, and we don't.
- **"Panopt must include a terminal emulator"** — no. Zellij is the emulator, and embedding `alacritty_terminal`/`vte`/`termwiz` inside a pane that's already running inside Zellij would be triple-nested emulation for no gain. The only thing it would buy us is decoupling from Zellij's keybinds, which is not worth the engineering cost.

**Where the commenter is genuinely right about a panopt gap**, even though their prescription overshoots:

Right now, drag-selection in an agent pane goes to WezTerm's screen-grid selection (the panopt code at `up.rs:152-153` comments on this explicitly: "globally-on mouse mode lets agent panes reach the terminal emulator's modifier-drag-to-select"). That selection cannot see Zellij pane boundaries and cannot see Zellij scrollback. So the failure mode the commenter describes — "drag past the pane edge or into off-screen scrollback" — is real for agent panes today.

The fix is not to write our own emulator. The fix is to bias users into **Zellij's** scroll mode for that use case, with two pieces of polish:

1. A friendlier entry binding than the default scroll-mode chord, plus visible affordance ("scroll/select mode" indicator).
2. Make sure `copy_command` is invoked from scroll-mode selection too, not just plugin-side selection — that's already true via Zellij's stock pipeline but worth verifying end-to-end now that the OSC 52 path is finally landing.

For **form panes** (todo/scratchpad editors), selection is already panopt-owned at the plugin layer, and OSC 52 export was just restored to work on drag release (`ccb8975`). That part is done.

So: thank the commenter for the framing, but the prescription is right architecture for the wrong project. Panopt's selection/copy story is Zellij scroll mode for agent panes + plugin-owned selection for form panes + OSC 52 egress to the WezTerm clipboard, and we're a couple of UX polish steps from that being the actual lived experience.

message.content: you mention ctrl-s to get into zellij scroll mode, but that isn't working in agent panes, at least.

input.description: Look at keybind setup in up.rs

content.text:
My mistake — panopt deliberately rewrites that binding. Look at `crates/panopt/src/up.rs:96-104`:

```rust
const SCROLL_BIND_FROM: &str = r#"bind "Ctrl s" { SwitchToMode "scroll"; }"#;
const SCROLL_BIND_TO:   &str = r#"bind "Ctrl Shift s" { SwitchToMode "scroll"; }"#;
```

`retarget_scroll_binding` moves scroll mode to **`Ctrl-Shift-S`** because the todo/scratchpad form claims `Ctrl-S` for save. So in a panopt cockpit, scroll mode is `Ctrl-Shift-S`, not `Ctrl-S`.

Let me check whether `Ctrl-Shift-S` is actually wired in the mode that agent panes are in (the cockpit auto-locks, so the locked-mode keybinds matter).

lastPrompt: you mention ctrl-s to get into zellij scroll mode, but that isn't working in agent panes, at least.

aiTitle: Implement pane-local terminal selection and copy

content.text:
Found the cause. The cockpit **auto-locks agent panes**, and the scroll-mode binding (whether stock `Ctrl-S` or panopt's retargeted `Ctrl-Shift-S`) lives in Zellij's `shared_except "locked"` block. That block is stripped under Locked mode.

`inject_locked_keybinds` (at `up.rs:625`) only adds Alt-arrows, Alt-N, and Ctrl-Q to the locked block — it deliberately does **not** include the scroll-mode entry. So in an agent pane right now:

- `Ctrl-S` — nothing (form save, but no form is focused)
- `Ctrl-Shift-S` — nothing (stripped in Locked)
- `PageUp` / `PageDown` — **works**, because `inject_page_scroll_keybinds` puts them in a `shared { ... }` block (`up.rs:719-724`) and `shared` applies to every mode including Locked. This pages through that pane's scrollback and auto-returns on next I/O.
- `Ctrl-G` then `Ctrl-Shift-S` — works, but two-step.

So the workable scrollback gesture today is PageUp/PageDown. Full pane-bounded selection with autoscroll requires the two-step (unlock first), which is what you're noticing.

The one-line fix is to add the scroll-mode binding to `locked_extra_binds`. Want me to wire it?

questions.question: How should scroll mode be reachable from an auto-locked agent pane?

options.label: Add Ctrl-Shift-S to locked
options.description: Splice `bind "Ctrl Shift s" { SwitchToMode "scroll"; }` into `locked_extra_binds`. Single keystroke from a locked agent pane enters Zellij scroll mode, where mouse drag selects pane-bounded with autoscroll and copy_command fires OSC 52. Same key as in unlocked modes — consistent mental model.

options.label: Add a different binding to locked
options.description: Pick a chord that doesn't collide with Claude Code (e.g. Alt-S, or a leader-style chord). Avoids any risk of Ctrl-Shift-S being eaten by something else, but means the gesture differs from unlocked panes.

options.label: Leave PageUp/PageDown as the answer
options.description: Document that PageUp/PageDown is the supported scrollback gesture in agent panes, and full mouse-drag selection requires Ctrl-G to unlock first. Smaller surface, no new keybinds, but no one-keystroke path to drag-select off-screen content.

content.content: User has answered your questions: "How should scroll mode be reachable from an auto-locked agent pane?"="1, but also: ctrl-s to save isn't a thing any more. those are now auto save.". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.

options.description: Splice `bind "Ctrl Shift s" { SwitchToMode "scroll"; }` into `locked_extra_binds`. Single keystroke from a locked agent pane enters Zellij scroll mode, where mouse drag selects pane-bounded with autoscroll and copy_command fires OSC 52. Same key as in unlocked modes — consistent mental model.

options.label: Add a different binding to locked
options.description: Pick a chord that doesn't collide with Claude Code (e.g. Alt-S, or a leader-style chord). Avoids any risk of Ctrl-Shift-S being eaten by something else, but means the gesture differs from unlocked panes.

options.label: Leave PageUp/PageDown as the answer
options.description: Document that PageUp/PageDown is the supported scrollback gesture in agent panes, and full mouse-drag selection requires Ctrl-G to unlock first. Smaller surface, no new keybinds, but no one-keystroke path to drag-select off-screen content.

answers.How should scroll mode be reachable from: 1, but also: ctrl-s to save isn't a thing any more. those are now auto save.

content.text: Good, that simplifies things. If forms auto-save, the whole reason for retargeting `Ctrl-S` to `Ctrl-Shift-S` is gone — I should restore stock `Ctrl-S` → scroll mode and bind that into the locked block. Let me verify forms no longer claim `Ctrl-S`.

lines: /// plugin auto-locks a content pane. Zellij's stock dump puts these in
/// `shared_except "locked"`, which strips them under Locked mode - without
/// this injection a user whose focused viewer / agent pane has just been
-/// auto-locked cannot move to another pane (Alt-arrows), spawn a new one
-/// (the cockpit's rewritten Alt-N), or quit (the rewritten Ctrl-Q) without
-/// first pressing Ctrl-G to escape - and the plugin would immediately
-/// re-lock them on the next PaneUpdate.
+/// auto-locked cannot enter scroll mode for pane-bounded selection (Ctrl-S),
+/// move to another pane (Alt-arrows), spawn a new one (the cockpit's
+/// rewritten Alt-N), or quit (the rewritten Ctrl-Q) without first pressing
+/// Ctrl-G to escape - and the plugin would immediately re-lock them on the
+/// next PaneUpdate.
/// Appending a *second* `keybinds { ... }` block does not work: Zellij only
/// reads the first top-level `keybinds` node and silently ignores the rest

Base directory for this skill: /home/gregz/cp/panopt/.claude/skills/verify-all

Run these two recipes in sequence and report results:

1. `just check` — `cargo check --workspace` plus `cargo check` on the plugin with `--target wasm32-wasip1`.
2. `just clippy` — same coverage but with `-D warnings`.

If clippy surfaces errors in code you didn't touch, call them out as pre-existing and do not auto-fix unless the user asks. Only fix warnings introduced by the current change.

Report format:
- One line per recipe: PASS or FAIL with a brief summary.
- For failures: include the first error block verbatim (file:line + message) and your read on whether it's from this session's changes or pre-existing.

Skip `just test` unless the user asks — it's slower and the user may want to gate test runs on a clean check/clippy first.

content.text:
- `just check` — **PASS**. Workspace + plugin both check clean.
- `just clippy` — **PASS**. Workspace + plugin both clippy clean under `-D warnings`.

## Summary

Made the change so `Ctrl-S` enters Zellij scroll mode in a focused, auto-locked agent pane (one keystroke, mouse-drag for pane-bounded selection with autoscroll, copy fires `copy_command` → OSC 52).

Files changed: `crates/panopt/src/up.rs` only.

- Removed `SCROLL_BIND_FROM`/`SCROLL_BIND_TO` constants, `retarget_scroll_binding` fn, its call site in `render_config`, the related warning, and its two unit tests. The retarget existed to free `Ctrl-S` for the form's save handler, which is moot now that the viewer drives debounced autosave.
- Added `bind "Ctrl s" { SwitchToMode "scroll"; }` to `locked_extra_binds`, so Zellij's stock binding stays in `shared_except "locked"` for non-locked panes and is also spliced into the locked block — Ctrl-S is the same single key in every mode.
- Updated three doc-comment blocks (the constants block, `render_config`, the embedded `// PANopt cockpit Zellij config` header) plus the `render_config-failed` warning, and the `inject_locked_keybinds_splices_into_first_locked_block` test to assert the new binding.

The pre-existing `mcp::tests::agent_mcp_json_is_valid_stdio_config` failure in `crates/panopt/src/mcp.rs:69` is unrelated (`${PANOPT_BIN:-panopt}` vs `"panopt"`).

You'll need `just refresh` to pick this up — the cockpit config is regenerated on `panopt up`, but the running daemon and existing Zellij session are running with the old keybinds.
